answer: Cynewulf wrote three poems in the corpus: (1) Andreas - A heroic narrative of Saint Andrew's missionary journey to convert the cannibalistic Mermedonians through faith and divine protection (cynewulf-andreas-07b573, chapter 1). (2) Elene - An account of Emperor Constantine's vision of the True Cross and the religious quest to discover and recover this sacred relic (cynewulf-elene-d2d132, chapter 1). (3) Juliana - A hagiographic poem depicting a Christian maiden's steadfast refusal to marry a pagan official and her unwavering commitment to her faith despite persecution (cynewulf-juliana-9a2157, chapter 1).
